feed.title: SAP Community - SAP Cloud Application Programming Model
feed.subtitle: SAP Cloud Application Programming Model Q&A in SAP Community

entry.title: How to connect and test CAP application locally with destinations using hybrid profile?
entry.content:
Hello supporters,
I do not want to deploy and test application, as developer, I would like to test app locally before push.
Running hybrid profile worked but returned empty array.

Why it shows empty array to me? What did I miss?

I am using Business Application Studio - BAS

It works in Postman.

cds watch --profile hybrid
cds serve all --with-mocks --in-memory? --profile hybrid 
live reload enabled for browsers
resolving cloud service bindings...
bound destinations to Cloud Foundry managed service opps-mta-destination:opps--destination-key
bound auth to Cloud Foundry managed service opps-mta-xsuaa:opps-mta-xsuaa-key
----
[cds] - mocking OPPS_DataAccessV4 { path: '/odata/v4/opps-data-access-v4' }
[cds] - server listening on { url: 'http://localhost:4004' }
----

 

Update: 
Is there anything wrong in my Run Configuration?

Why I don't see Live Data option also?  based on Run Config documentation.

Step 1 try: 

I turned the option to -> "Mock external OData services (Off)"

[cds] - connect to db > sqlite {url: ':memory:'}
/> successfully deployed to in-memory database. 
Process exited with code 1
Error: No credentials configured for "OPPS_DataAccessV4".
    at RemoteService.init (/home/user/projects/opps-promotions-api-mta/node_modules/@sap/cds/libx/_runtime/remote/Service.js:223:13)

When clicked on 3rd option "OPPS_DataAccessV4 - OData" in order to Bind it, then got strange error:

Step 2 try:

I turned the option to ->  "Mock external OData services (On)" and I got error:

Process exited with code 1
[cds] - using auth strategy {kind: 'xsuaa', impl: 'node_modules/@sap/cds/lib/auth/jwt-auth'} 
Error: Authentication kind "xsuaa" configured, but no XSUAA instance bound to application. Either bind an XSUAA instance, or switch to an authentication kind that does not require a binding.

How to fix this?

We really need to solve this. Please support, Thanks.

@vvdries @gregorw @qmacro @martinfrick @martinstenzig @WouterLemaire @Marian_Zeis 
@showkath_naseem @LucasBorin @massimiliano_cardosi @rotem_puterman-sobe @MariamIdman

I referred these posts: 

https://community.sap.com/t5/technology-blogs-by-sap/btp-cap-how-to-connect-to-remote-services-locally-in-cap-application/ba-p/13550070

https://community.sap.com/t5/technology-blogs-by-members/sap-cap-hybrid-testing/ba-p/13556582

https://community.sap.com/t5/technology-q-a/btp-cap-connect-to-remote-service-locally-hybrid/qaq-p/12662393

entry.title: Generate CAP Project in BAS with Error @sap/hdi-deploy@5.4.2 Not Match
entry.content:
Today, I will generate CAP project via create from template wizard but error @SAP/hdi-deploy@5.4.2 not match

and can't build mta file as picture below.

entry.title: Connect to ldap server in CAP (nodeJS) project on BTP
entry.content:
Hi all,
I need to connect to ldap server on CAP project with nodeJS.
Locally, I use the ldapjs library and everything works correctly. The problem is when I try to use it from the BAS or deploy the project to BTP - SAP BTP Cloud Foundry doesn’t allow direct access to arbitrary external systems unless the connection is explicitly tunneled through the Cloud Connector and Destination Service.
So I try to create a cloud connector and destination but It doesn't work like I'm expecting. I don't how can i create the connection (ldap.createClient) with the destination and of course how can I create/search entries.

Someone maybe use ldap with CAP and BTP before and can explain me, step by step, what should I do the make it work ?

Thank you !

In addition, this is my locally ldap file that works correctly in locally:

require('dotenv').config();

const cds = require('@sap/cds');
const ldap = require('ldapjs');

module.exports = cds.service.impl(async function () {

const { LDAP_USER, LDAP_PASSWORD } = process.env;

const createConnection = async (server) => {
const client = ldap.createClient({ url: `ldap://${server}`, port: 636, reconnect: true });

return new Promise((resolve, reject) => {
client.bind(LDAP_USER, LDAP_PASSWORD, (err) => {

if (err) {
client.unbind();
return reject(new Error("LDAP bind failed"));
}

console.log("LDAP bind successful");
resolve(client);
});
});
};

this.on("getNacEntry", async (req) => {
const { server, macAddress } = req.data;

if (!macAddress) return req.error(400, "Mac-Address is required");

return new Promise(async (resolve, reject) => {
const client = await createConnection(server);

const scope = 'ou=PXE-Boot,ou=MACEntries,ou=external,o=SAP';
const filter = `(cn=${macAddress})`;

const options = { scope: 'sub', filter };

client.search(scope, options, (err, res) => {
if (err) {
client.unbind();
return reject(req.error(500, 'Search failed: ' + err));
}

let result = null;

res.on('searchEntry', (entry) => {
result = entry.pojo.attributes;
});

res.on('end', () => {
client.unbind();
if (result) resolve({ code: 200, message: `Mac-Address ${macAddress} found in MacEntries on ${server}` });
else reject(req.error(404, `Mac-Address ${macAddress} not found in MacEntries on ${server}`));
});

res.on('error', (err) => {
client.unbind();
reject(req.error(500, 'LDAP search error: ' + err.message));
});
});
});
});

this.on("getNacGroup", async (req) => {
const { server, macAddress } = req.data;

if (!macAddress) return req.error(400, "Mac-Address is required");

return new Promise(async (resolve, reject) => {
const client = await createConnection(server);

const scope = 'cn=PXE-Boot,ou=MACGroups,ou=external,o=SAP'; // Base DN
const filter = '(cn=PXE-Boot)'; // LDAP filter
const attributes = ['uniqueMember']; // Only fetch uniqueMember attribute

const opts = { scope: 'sub', filter, attributes };

client.search(scope, opts, (err, res) => {
if (err) {
client.unbind();
return reject(req.error(500, 'Search failed: ' + err));
}

let uniqueMember = null;

res.on('searchEntry', (entry) => {
if (entry.pojo) {
const uniqueObject = entry.pojo.attributes.find(item => item.type === "uniqueMember");

uniqueMember = uniqueObject.values.find(member =>
member.toUpperCase().startsWith(`CN=${macAddress},`)
);
}
});

res.on('end', () => {
client.unbind();

if (uniqueMember) resolve({ code: 200, message: `Mac-Address ${macAddress} found in MacGroups on ${server}` });
else reject(req.error(404, `Mac-Address ${macAddress} not found in MacGroups on ${server}`));
});

res.on('error', (err) => {
client.unbind();
reject(req.error(500, 'LDAP search error: ' + err.message));
});
});
});
});

this.on("setNacEntry", async (req) => {
const { server, macAddress } = req.data;

if (!macAddress) return req.error(400, "Mac-Address is required");

return new Promise(async (resolve, reject) => {
const client = await createConnection(server);

// Convert MAC address into multiple formats
const macArray = macAddress.split(':');
const macWithout = macArray.join('');
const macDash = macArray.join('-');
const macQuadColon = `${macArray[0]}${macArray[1]}:${macArray[2]}${macArray[3]}:${macArray[4]}${macArray[5]}`;
const macQuadDot = `${macArray[0]}${macArray[1]}.${macArray[2]}${macArray[3]}.${macArray[4]}${macArray[5]}`;
const macQuadDash = `${macArray[0]}${macArray[1]}-${macArray[2]}${macArray[3]}-${macArray[4]}${macArray[5]}`;

const macAll = [macAddress, macWithout, macDash, macQuadColon, macQuadDot, macQuadDash];

// LDAP entry data
const dn = `cn=${macAddress},ou=PXE-Boot,ou=MACEntries,ou=external,o=SAP`;
const entry = {
sn: 'device',
cn: macAll,
macAddress: macAll,
objectClass: ['ieee802Device', 'person']
};

// Perform the LDAP Add operation
client.add(dn, entry, (err) => {
client.unbind(); // Close connection

if (!err) return resolve({ code: 200, message: "Mac-Address added to MacEntries successfully" });

// Handle specific LDAP errors
if (err.lde_message === "Entry Already Exists") {
return reject(req.error(409, "The object already exists."));
} else {
return reject(req.error(500, "LDAP error: " + err.message));
}
});
})
});

this.on("setNacGroup", async (req) => {
const { server, macAddress } = req.data;

if (!macAddress) return req.error(400, "Mac-Address is required");

return new Promise(async (resolve, reject) => {
const client = await createConnection(server);

const uniqueMember = `cn=${macAddress},ou=PXE-Boot,ou=MACEntries,ou=external,o=SAP`;
const groupDN = "cn=PXE-Boot,ou=MACGroups,ou=external,o=SAP";

const change = new ldap.Change({
operation: 'add',
modification: new ldap.Attribute({
type: 'uniqueMember',
values: [uniqueMember]
})
});

// Perform LDAP Modify request
client.modify(groupDN, change, (err) => {
client.unbind(); // Close connection

if (!err) return resolve({ code: 200, message: "Mac-Address added to MACGroups successfully" });

// Handle specific LDAP errors
if (err.lde_message === "Attribute Or Value Exists") {
return reject(req.error(409, "The specified directory service attribute or value already exists."));
} else {
return reject(req.error(500, "LDAP error: " + err.message));
}
});
});
});

this.on("deleteNacEntry", async (req) => {
const { server, macAddress } = req.data;

if (!macAddress) return req.error(400, "Mac-Address is required");

return new Promise(async (resolve, reject) => {
const client = await createConnection(server);

const baseDN = 'ou=PXE-Boot,ou=MACEntries,ou=external,o=SAP';
const filter = `(cn=${macAddress})`;

const searchOptions = { scope: 'sub', filter };

// Search for the MAC address
client.search(baseDN, searchOptions, (err, res) => {
if (err) {
client.unbind();
return reject(req.error(500, "LDAP search failed: " + err.message));
}

let distinguishedName = null;

res.on('searchEntry', (entry) => {
distinguishedName = entry.pojo.objectName; // Get DN from entry
});

res.on('end', () => {
if (!distinguishedName) {
client.unbind();
return reject(req.error(404, `Mac-Address ${macAddress} not found in MacEntries`));
}

// Perform the delete operation
client.del(distinguishedName, (err) => {
client.unbind();
if (err) {
return reject(req.error(500, "LDAP delete failed: " + err.message));
}
return resolve({ code: 200, message: "Mac-Address deleted from MacEntries successfully" });
});
});

res.on('error', (err) => {
client.unbind();
reject(req.error(500, "LDAP search error: " + err.message));
});
});
});
});

this.on("deleteNacFromGroup", async (req) => {
const { server, macAddress } = req.data;

if (!macAddress) return req.error(400, "Mac-Address is required");

return new Promise(async (resolve, reject) => {
const client = await createConnection(server);

const groupDN = "cn=PXE-Boot,ou=MACGroups,ou=external,o=SAP";
const searchOptions = { scope: 'sub', attributes: ['uniqueMember'] };

// Search for the MAC address in MACGroups
client.search(groupDN, searchOptions, (err, res) => {
if (err) {
client.unbind();
return reject(req.error(500, "LDAP search failed: " + err.message));
}

let uniqueMember = null;

res.on('searchEntry', (entry) => {
if (entry.pojo) {
const uniqueObject = entry.pojo.attributes.find(item => item.type === "uniqueMember");

uniqueMember = uniqueObject.values.find(member =>
member.toUpperCase().startsWith(`CN=${macAddress},`)
);
}

});

res.on('end', () => {
if (!uniqueMember) {
client.unbind();
return reject(req.error(404, `Mac-Address ${macAddress} not found in MacGroups`));
}

const change = new ldap.Change({
operation: 'delete',
modification: new ldap.Attribute({
type: 'uniqueMember',
values: [uniqueMember]
})
});

// Perform LDAP Modify request to remove MAC from group
client.modify(groupDN, change, (err) => {
client.unbind();
if (err) {
return reject(req.error(500, "LDAP modify failed: " + err.message));
}
return resolve({ code: 200, message: "Mac-Address removed from MACGroups successfully" });
});
});

res.on('error', (err) => {
client.unbind();
reject(req.error(500, "LDAP search error: " + err.message));
});
});
});
});
});

entry.title: com/sap/cloud/mt/subscription/exits/SubscribeExit.class file not found after CDS Feb 2025 upgrade
entry.content:
Hi team,

I have upgraded my java backend application to CDS February 2025 version. 

I am using 3.8.0 cds version inside pom.xml.

However, I am getting following error after the upgrade when deploying my application with cf push:
 
org.springframework.beans.factory.BeanDefinitionStoreException: I/O failure while processing configuration class [com.sap.xyz.v2.lib.manageddb.exits.SubscribeExit]
org.springframework.context.annotation.ConfigurationClassParser.processConfigurationClass(ConfigurationClassParser.java:251)
org.springframework.context.annotation.ConfigurationClassParser.parse(ConfigurationClassParser.java:189)
org.springframework.context.annotation.ConfigurationClassParser.doProcessConfigurationClass(ConfigurationClassParser.java:314)
org.springframework.context.annotation.ConfigurationClassParser.processConfigurationClass(ConfigurationClassParser.java:246)
org.springframework.context.annotation.ConfigurationClassParser.parse(ConfigurationClassParser.java:197)
org.springframework.context.annotation.ConfigurationClassParser.parse(ConfigurationClassParser.java:165)
org.springframework.context.annotation.ConfigurationClassPostProcessor.processConfigBeanDefinitions(ConfigurationClassPostProcessor.java:417)
org.springframework.context.annotation.ConfigurationClassPostProcessor.postProcessBeanDefinitionRegistry(ConfigurationClassPostProcessor.java:290)
org.springframework.context.support.PostProcessorRegistrationDelegate.invokeBeanDefinitionRegistryPostProcessors(PostProcessorRegistrationDelegate.java:349)
org.springframework.context.support.PostProcessorRegistrationDelegate.invokeBeanFactoryPostProcessors(PostProcessorRegistrationDelegate.java:118)
org.springframework.context.support.AbstractApplicationContext.invokeBeanFactoryPostProcessors(AbstractApplicationContext.java:789)
org.springframework.context.support.AbstractApplicationContext.refresh(AbstractApplicationContext.java:607)
org.springframework.boot.web.servlet.context.ServletWebServerApplicationContext.refresh(ServletWebServerApplicationContext.java:146)
org.springframework.boot.SpringApplication.refresh(SpringApplication.java:754)
org.springframework.boot.SpringApplication.refreshContext(SpringApplication.java:456)
org.springframework.boot.SpringApplication.run(SpringApplication.java:335)
org.springframework.boot.SpringApplication.run(SpringApplication.java:1363)
org.springframework.boot.SpringApplication.run(SpringApplication.java:1352)
com.sap.xyz.v2.renn.bpp.BPApplication.main(BPApplication.java:12)
java.base/jdk.internal.reflect.NativeMethodAccessorImpl.invoke0(Native Method)
java.base/jdk.internal.reflect.NativeMethodAccessorImpl.invoke(Unknown Source)
java.base/jdk.internal.reflect.DelegatingMethodAccessorImpl.invoke(Unknown Source)
java.base/java.lang.reflect.Method.invoke(Unknown Source)
org.springframework.boot.loader.launch.Launcher.launch(Launcher.java:91)
org.springframework.boot.loader.launch.Launcher.launch(Launcher.java:53)
org.springframework.boot.loader.launch.JarLauncher.main(JarLauncher.java:58)
Caused by: java.io.FileNotFoundException: class path resource [com/sap/cloud/mt/subscription/exits/SubscribeExit.class] cannot be opened because it does not exist
org.springframework.core.io.ClassPathResource.getInputStream(ClassPathResource.java:215)
org.springframework.core.type.classreading.SimpleMetadataReader.getClassReader(SimpleMetadataReader.java:54)
org.springframework.core.type.classreading.SimpleMetadataReader.<init>(SimpleMetadataReader.java:48)
org.springframework.core.type.classreading.SimpleMetadataReaderFactory.getMetadataReader(SimpleMetadataReaderFactory.java:103)
org.springframework.boot.type.classreading.ConcurrentReferenceCachingMetadataReaderFactory.createMetadataReader(ConcurrentReferenceCachingMetadataReaderFactory.java:86)
org.springframework.boot.type.classreading.ConcurrentReferenceCachingMetadataReaderFactory.getMetadataReader(ConcurrentReferenceCachingMetadataReaderFactory.java:73)
org.springframework.core.type.classreading.SimpleMetadataReaderFactory.getMetadataReader(SimpleMetadataReaderFactory.java:81)
org.springframework.context.annotation.ConfigurationClassParser.asSourceClass(ConfigurationClassParser.java:630)
org.springframework.context.annotation.ConfigurationClassParser$SourceClass.getInterfaces(ConfigurationClassParser.java:956)
org.springframework.context.annotation.ConfigurationClassParser.processInterfaces(ConfigurationClassParser.java:396)
org.springframework.context.annotation.ConfigurationClassParser.doProcessConfigurationClass(ConfigurationClassParser.java:342)
org.springframework.context.annotation.ConfigurationClassParser.processConfigurationClass(ConfigurationClassParser.java:246)
... 25 common frames omitted
Exit status 1
 

When I encountered this error, I also upgraded com.sap.cloud.mt > orm version to 3.3.5 (latest).

I am still facing the same issue.

Any help on this would be appreciated.

entry.title: Re: Annotations with Dynamic values to make CRUD operatio...
entry.content:
I found the following example by SAP beneficial: Developing an SAP Fiori Elements App Based on a CAP OData V4 Service: Applying Generic Actions Provided by SAP Fiori Elements

For "Capabilities.DeleteRestrictionsType.Deletable", you need to use a property with a boolean data type, but has an integer value (0 = false, 1 = true).

For "Capabilities.UpdateRestrictions.Updatable", you need to use a property with a boolean data type and has a boolean value.

entry.title: 403 Forbidden Error When Sending Email via SAP Cloud SDK from SAP Build Work Zone
entry.content:
Hi SAP Experts,

I have developed and deployed an SAP BTP CAP application on the SAP BTP Cloud Foundry environment and integrated it with SAP Build Work Zone, Standard Edition.

I am using the SAP Cloud SDK (@SAP-cloud-sdk/mail-client) to send emails from my SAP BTP CAP application. The email functionality works perfectly when testing locally using SAP Business Application Studio (BAS) or when accessing the application directly in the Cloud Foundry environment in SAP BTP.

However, when I open the application and try to send an email through SAP Build Work Zone, Standard Edition, I encounter a 403 Forbidden error while sending an email.

I have already added the necessary routes in the xs-app.json file, but the issue persists.

Has anyone faced a similar issue? Are there additional configurations required for SAP Build Work Zone to allow outbound email requests?

Any insights or suggestions would be greatly appreciated!

xs-app.json:

xs-app.json
{
  "welcomeFile": "/index.html",
  "authenticationMethod": "route",
  "routes": [
 {
      "source": "^/MailService/(.*)$",
      "target": "/MailService/$1",
      "destination": "srv",
      "authenticationType": "xsuaa",
      "csrfProtection": false
    }
  ]
}

srv > mail.cds:

@protocol: 'rest'
@path    : '/MailService'
service Catalog {

   @open
   type object {};
action sendemail();
}

Error:

Regards,
Syed

entry.title: How to extend headers in fiori list object
entry.content: I am creating a CAP application with fiori UI using list object floorplan. I want to extend my header to add one more custom heading. is it possible ?

entry.title: How to Pass Large String to Action/Function in CAP Java
entry.content:
I define an Action in CDS view within CAP Java Framework.

action testorchestrate(sysmessage:String, usermessage:String, image:String) returns String;

Then I access the API like below:

POST /testorchestrate

{
"sysmessage": "you are an assitant",
"usermessage": "what you see",
"image":  ...long string
}
 
It returns error, how to deal with this issue?

Message Request header is too large

Description The server cannot or will not process the request due to something that is perceived to be a client error (e.g., malformed request syntax, invalid request message framing, or deceptive request routing).

Exception

java.lang.IllegalArgumentException: Request header is too large
	org.apache.coyote.http11.Http11InputBuffer.fill(Http11InputBuffer.java:777)
	org.apache.coyote.http11.Http11InputBuffer.parseHeader(Http11InputBuffer.java:971)
	org.apache.coyote.http11.Http11InputBuffer.parseHeaders(Http11InputBuffer.java:592)
	org.apache.coyote.http11.Http11Processor.service(Http11Processor.java:293)
	org.apache.coyote.AbstractProcessorLight.process(AbstractProcessorLight.java:63)
	org.apache.coyote.AbstractProtocol$ConnectionHandler.process(AbstractProtocol.java:905)
	org.apache.tomcat.util.net.NioEndpoint$SocketProcessor.doRun(NioEndpoint.java:1741)
	org.apache.tomcat.util.net.SocketProcessorBase.run(SocketProcessorBase.java:52)
	org.apache.tomcat.util.threads.ThreadPoolExecutor.runWorker(ThreadPoolExecutor.java:1190)
	org.apache.tomcat.util.threads.ThreadPoolExecutor$Worker.run(ThreadPoolExecutor.java:659)
	org.apache.tomcat.util.threads.TaskThread$Wrappin

entry.title: How to call Function Import of external OData for fetching PDF in CAPM application
entry.content:
I am developing CAP app on BAS to fetch PDF file of billing document. I am consuming standard OData API_BILLING_DOCUMENT_SRV in CAPM, I am able to get entities data but on using function Import GetPDF, I always get undefined even though terminal shows no error. Please help.

this is the edmx definition of function import.

 

<FunctionImport Name="GetPDF" ReturnType="API_BILLING_DOCUMENT_SRV.GetPDFResult" m:HttpMethod="GET" sap:action-for="API_BILLING_DOCUMENT_SRV.A_BillingDocumentType"><Parameter Name="BillingDocument" Type="Edm.String" Mode="In" MaxLength="11000"/></FunctionImport>
 
Below is my code
service.js
=======
 
const cds = require('@sap/cds');
 
module.exports = cds.service.impl(async function () {
    const {BD} = this.entities;
    const BDService = await cds.connect.to('API_BILLING_DOCUMENT_SRV');
 
    this.on('READ', 'BD', async (req) => {
        return await BDService.run(SELECT.from(BD)); // Successfully connects and returns data from entity
    });

    this.on('getFile',  async (req) => {
        const {docNo} = req.data;
        const parameters = { BillingDocument: docNo };
        const headers = { "Accept" : "application/json"  };
 
        const result = await BDService.send("GetPDF",parameters,headers);
        return result; // undefined sadly, expecting binary pdf string, directly calling api loads PDF data
    });
 
});
 
service.cds
=========
 
using {API_BILLING_DOCUMENT_SRV} from './external/API_BILLING_DOCUMENT_SRV.csn';

service BillingDocumentService {

  entity BD as select from API_BILLING_DOCUMENT_SRV.A_BillingDocument;

  function getFile(docNo : String) returns API_BILLING_DOCUMENT_SRV.GetPDFResult;
 
}

I have referred below github for using function import

https://github.com/SAP-samples/btp-extension-s4-material-availability/blob/main/srv/service.js

Below is documentation

https://help.sap.com/docs/SAP_S4HANA_CLOUD/03c04db2a7434731b7fe21dca77440da/990f1ce2078f42e4b593d6d060966bd8.html

entry.title: add toolbar action on a table to update all rows
entry.content:
In the Fiori docu, the scenario of updateing selected rows of a table before updating is described in the docu.

I like to have a toolbar action in the table toolbar in order to preset field values in all rows of a table without the need to select all table rows. Is this supported by Fiori Elements ? 

I tried the following which does not work:

a) definition of my service

service AdminConfigurationService {
  ...
  entity SystemKinds       as projection on paltr.BaseSystemKinds;
  action   setSystemKindsInitialLanguages();
}

b) service:

class AdminConfigurationService extends cds.ApplicationService {

async init() {
  const { SystemKinds } = this.entities;
  ...
   if (!!SystemKinds.texts) {
      this.on('setSystemKindsInitialLanguages', async (req) => {
        let { newTitle, ID } = req.data;

        if (!!SystemKinds.texts?.drafts) {
          await this.update(SystemKinds.texts.drafts, ID).with({ title: newTitle });
        }
      });
    }
    await super.init();
  }
}

c) CustomActionController:

import ExtensionAPI from 'sap/fe/core/ExtensionAPI';
import Context from 'sap/ui/model/odata/v4/Context';
import Dialog from 'sap/m/Dialog';
import Event from 'sap/ui/base/Event';
import Input from 'sap/m/Input';
import Button from 'sap/m/Button';
import ODataModel from 'sap/ui/model/odata/v4/ODataModel';
import MessageToast from 'sap/m/MessageToast';
import Fragment from 'sap/ui/core/Fragment';

/**
 * Generated event handler.
 *
 * @Param this reference to the 'this' that the event handler is bound to.
 * @Param pageContext the context of the page on which the event was fired
 */

export async function SetTitles(this: ExtensionAPI, pageContext: Context) {
    let fragDialog: Dialog;


    fragDialog = <Dialog>await this.loadFragment({
        id: "paltr_fragmentSystemKindsInitialLanguages",
        name: "paltr.core.configsystemkind.ext.CustomDialogInitialLanguages",
        controller: this
    });

    fragDialog.getBeginButton().attachPress(pressInitialLanguagesSubmit, this);
    fragDialog.getEndButton().attachPress(pressInitialLanguagesCancel, this);

    fragDialog.open();
}

export async function pressInitialLanguagesSubmit(this: ExtensionAPI, pressEvent: Event): Promise<void> {
    const title = (Fragment.byId("paltr_fragmentSystemKindsInitialLanguages", "Paltr_CustomDialogInitialLanguagesInput") as Input).getValue(),
        actionName = "setSystemKindsInitialLanguages",
        context = this.getEditFlow().getView().getBindingContext() as Context;

    let mParameters = {        
        model: this.getEditFlow().getView().getModel() as ODataModel,
        parameterValues: [{
            newTitle: title,            
        }],
        requiresNavigation: false,
        skipParameterDialog: true
    };

    await this.getEditFlow().invokeAction(actionName, mParameters); //SAP Fiori elements EditFlow API

    const dialog = (pressEvent.getSource() as Button).getParent() as Dialog;
    dialog.close();
    dialog.destroy();
    MessageToast.show("Werte gesetzt");
}

export function pressInitialLanguagesCancel(this: ExtensionAPI, pressEvent: Event): void {
    const dialog = (pressEvent.getSource() as Button).getParent() as Dialog;
    dialog.close();
    dialog.destroy();
}

d) fragement:

<core:FragmentDefinition 
xmlns="sap.m" 
xmlns:core="sap.ui.core" 
xmlns:mvc="sap.ui.core.mvc" 
xmlns:macros="sap.fe.macros">
	<Dialog id="paltr_dialogSystemKindsInitialLanguages" title="{i18n>Paltr_CustomDialogInitialLanguagesHeader}" class="sapUiResponsivePadding--header sapUiResponsivePadding--content" >
		<HBox alignItems="Center" alignContent="Center">			
			<Label text="{i18n>Paltr_CustomDialogInitialLanguagesLabel}" class="sapUiSmallMarginEnd" labelFor="Paltr_CustomDialogInitialLanguagesInput"/>
			<Input value="{viewModel>/defaulttext}" id="Paltr_CustomDialogInitialLanguagesInput" maxLength="100" width="30rem"  placeholder="{i18n>Paltr_CustomDialogInitialLanguagesPlaceholder}"/> 
		</HBox>
		<beginButton>
			<Button text="{i18n>Paltr_CustomDialogInitialLanguagesApply}" id="Paltr_CustomDialogInitialLanguagesButtonApply" />
		</beginButton>
		<endButton>
			<Button text="{i18n>Paltr_CustomDialogInitialLanguagesCancel}" id="Paltr_CustomDialogInitialLanguagesButtonCancel"/>
		</endButton>
	</Dialog>
</core:FragmentDefinition>

 

Running this, I'm able to press the action button (in my case "Übersetzungen machen") and enter a new title in the dialog popup which should be set to all rows in the table.

If I confirm the dialog with "Anwenden", I will immediately get the error :

(A draft version can only be modified by its associated root entity).
Setting a breakpoint in the service class behind "this.on(....)" will not be reached.
What is my error? How should I implement an action in order to update all rows of a table without selecting the table rows before?

Yours
Stephan

entry.title: Generate IDOC from SAP BTP CAP app
entry.content:
Hi Experts,

For one of the requirements, we need to generate a IDOC in SAP ECC system from SAP BTP CAP application. Can you please give any idea how we can achieve it?

entry.title: CAP Java App: Subscription Failed on BTP Cloud Foudary
entry.content:
Hi,

I was deploying my CAP Java App called "plugin-demo" on Cloud Foundry by following the official document: Deploy to Cloud Foundry | capire step by step (I don't have a portal so I jumped step 5). But after the apps are deployed successfully:

When I subscribed it through another account, this error shows up: 

Please open a support case for the owner of application/service 'plugin-demo' with appName: plugin-demo-SAP_metering-metering_cap_test_org. It failed its asynchronous callback and responded with message: Subscription failed.

And when I inspected the logs in my apps:

the logs in plugin-demo-srv:

2025-04-07T10:51:32.153+0000 [APP/PROC/WEB/0] STDOUT 2025-04-07T10:51:32.146Z ERROR 7 --- [       Thread-4] c.s.c.a.s.SaasProvisioningServlet        : Subscription for tenant '35346b0c-194e-484b-8173-698efda8649b' failed
2025-04-07T10:51:32.153+0000 [APP/PROC/WEB/0] STDOUT com.sap.cds.services.impl.ContextualizedServiceException: Failed to process subscription of tenant '35346b0c-194e-484b-8173-698efda8649b' (service 'MtSubscriptionService$Default', event 'ASYNC_SUBSCRIBE', entity '<no entity>')
2025-04-07T10:51:32.153+0000 [APP/PROC/WEB/0] STDOUT at com.sap.cds.services.impl.ServiceImpl.dispatch(ServiceImpl.java:255) ~[cds-services-impl-2.7.0.jar:na]
2025-04-07T10:51:32.153+0000 [APP/PROC/WEB/0] STDOUT at com.sap.cds.services.impl.ServiceImpl.emit(ServiceImpl.java:176) ~[cds-services-impl-2.7.0.jar:na]
2025-04-07T10:51:32.153+0000 [APP/PROC/WEB/0] STDOUT at com.sap.cds.services.ServiceDelegator.emit(ServiceDelegator.java:33) ~[cds-services-api-2.7.0.jar:na]
2025-04-07T10:51:32.153+0000 [APP/PROC/WEB/0] STDOUT at com.sap.cds.services.mt.impl.MtSubscriptionServiceCompatibilityHandler.onSubscribe(MtSubscriptionServiceCompatibilityHandler.java:203) ~[cds-feature-mt-2.7.0.jar:na]
2025-04-07T10:51:32.153+0000 [APP/PROC/WEB/0] STDOUT at com.sap.cds.services.impl.handlerregistry.HandlerRegistryTools$DescribedHandler.process(HandlerRegistryTools.java:165) ~[cds-services-impl-2.7.0.jar:na]
2025-04-07T10:51:32.153+0000 [APP/PROC/WEB/0] STDOUT at com.sap.cds.services.impl.ServiceImpl.dispatch(ServiceImpl.java:233) ~[cds-services-impl-2.7.0.jar:na]
2025-04-07T10:51:32.153+0000 [APP/PROC/WEB/0] STDOUT at com.sap.cds.services.impl.ServiceImpl.lambda$dispatchInChangeSetContext$2(ServiceImpl.java:202) ~[cds-services-impl-2.7.0.jar:na]
2025-04-07T10:51:32.153+0000 [APP/PROC/WEB/0] STDOUT at com.sap.cds.services.impl.runtime.ChangeSetContextRunnerImpl.lambda$run$1(ChangeSetContextRunnerImpl.java:48) ~[cds-services-impl-2.7.0.jar:na]
2025-04-07T10:51:32.153+0000 [APP/PROC/WEB/0] STDOUT at com.sap.cds.services.impl.runtime.ChangeSetContextRunnerImpl.open(ChangeSetContextRunnerImpl.java:62) ~[cds-services-impl-2.7.0.jar:na]
2025-04-07T10:51:32.153+0000 [APP/PROC/WEB/0] STDOUT at com.sap.cds.services.impl.runtime.ChangeSetContextRunnerImpl.run(ChangeSetContextRunnerImpl.java:41) ~[cds-services-impl-2.7.0.jar:na]
2025-04-07T10:51:32.153+0000 [APP/PROC/WEB/0] STDOUT at com.sap.cds.services.impl.runtime.ChangeSetContextRunnerImpl.run(ChangeSetContextRunnerImpl.java:47) ~[cds-services-impl-2.7.0.jar:na]
2025-04-07T10:51:32.153+0000 [APP/PROC/WEB/0] STDOUT at com.sap.cds.services.impl.ServiceImpl.dispatchInChangeSetContext(ServiceImpl.java:202) ~[cds-services-impl-2.7.0.jar:na]
2025-04-07T10:51:32.153+0000 [APP/PROC/WEB/0] STDOUT at com.sap.cds.services.impl.ServiceImpl.emit(ServiceImpl.java:187) ~[cds-services-impl-2.7.0.jar:na]
2025-04-07T10:51:32.153+0000 [APP/PROC/WEB/0] STDOUT at com.sap.cds.services.ServiceDelegator.emit(ServiceDelegator.java:33) ~[cds-services-api-2.7.0.jar:na]
2025-04-07T10:51:32.153+0000 [APP/PROC/WEB/0] STDOUT at com.sap.cds.services.impl.mt.DeploymentServiceImpl.subscribe(DeploymentServiceImpl.java:40) ~[cds-services-impl-2.7.0.jar:na]
2025-04-07T10:51:32.153+0000 [APP/PROC/WEB/0] STDOUT at com.sap.cds.adapter.subscription.SaasProvisioningServlet.lambda$doPut$7(SaasProvisioningServlet.java:182) ~[cds-feature-mt-2.7.0.jar:na]
2025-04-07T10:51:32.153+0000 [APP/PROC/WEB/0] STDOUT at com.sap.cds.feature.mt.ExecutorUtils.lambda$runAsynchronously$0(ExecutorUtils.java:21) ~[cds-feature-mt-2.7.0.jar:na]
2025-04-07T10:51:32.153+0000 [APP/PROC/WEB/0] STDOUT at com.sap.cds.services.impl.runtime.RequestContextRunnerImpl.lambda$run$3(RequestContextRunnerImpl.java:213) ~[cds-services-impl-2.7.0.jar:na]
2025-04-07T10:51:32.153+0000 [APP/PROC/WEB/0] STDOUT at com.sap.cds.services.impl.runtime.RequestContextRunnerImpl.run(RequestContextRunnerImpl.java:272) ~[cds-services-impl-2.7.0.jar:na]
2025-04-07T10:51:32.153+0000 [APP/PROC/WEB/0] STDOUT at com.sap.cds.services.impl.runtime.RequestContextRunnerImpl.run(RequestContextRunnerImpl.java:212) ~[cds-services-impl-2.7.0.jar:na]
2025-04-07T10:51:32.153+0000 [APP/PROC/WEB/0] STDOUT at com.sap.cds.feature.mt.ExecutorUtils.lambda$runAsynchronously$1(ExecutorUtils.java:20) ~[cds-feature-mt-2.7.0.jar:na]
2025-04-07T10:51:32.153+0000 [APP/PROC/WEB/0] STDOUT at java.base/java.lang.Thread.run(Thread.java:840) ~[na:na]
2025-04-07T10:51:32.153+0000 [APP/PROC/WEB/0] STDOUT Caused by: com.sap.cds.services.utils.ErrorStatusException: Failed to process subscription of tenant '35346b0c-194e-484b-8173-698efda8649b'
2025-04-07T10:51:32.153+0000 [APP/PROC/WEB/0] STDOUT at com.sap.cds.services.mt.impl.MtSubscriptionServiceCompatibilityHandler.onSubscribe(MtSubscriptionServiceCompatibilityHandler.java:136) ~[cds-feature-mt-2.7.0.jar:na]
2025-04-07T10:51:32.153+0000 [APP/PROC/WEB/0] STDOUT at com.sap.cds.services.mt.impl.MtSubscriptionServiceCompatibilityHandler.onSubscribeAsync(MtSubscriptionServiceCompatibilityHandler.java:159) ~[cds-feature-mt-2.7.0.jar:na]
2025-04-07T10:51:32.153+0000 [APP/PROC/WEB/0] STDOUT at com.sap.cds.services.impl.handlerregistry.HandlerRegistryTools$DescribedHandler.process(HandlerRegistryTools.java:165) ~[cds-services-impl-2.7.0.jar:na]
2025-04-07T10:51:32.153+0000 [APP/PROC/WEB/0] STDOUT at com.sap.cds.services.impl.ServiceImpl.dispatch(ServiceImpl.java:233) ~[cds-services-impl-2.7.0.jar:na]
2025-04-07T10:51:32.153+0000 [APP/PROC/WEB/0] STDOUT ... 21 common frames omitted
2025-04-07T10:51:32.153+0000 [APP/PROC/WEB/0] STDOUT Caused by: com.sap.cloud.mt.subscription.exceptions.InternalError: Unexpected return code 404
2025-04-07T10:51:32.153+0000 [APP/PROC/WEB/0] STDOUT at com.sap.cloud.mt.subscription.SidecarAccess.lambda$new$2(SidecarAccess.java:89) ~[multi-tenant-subscription-2.7.0.jar:na]
2025-04-07T10:51:32.153+0000 [APP/PROC/WEB/0] STDOUT at com.sap.cloud.mt.tools.api.ServiceEndpoint$ServiceEndpointBuilder.lambda$createResponseChecker$0(ServiceEndpoint.java:93) ~[tools-2.7.0.jar:na]
2025-04-07T10:51:32.153+0000 [APP/PROC/WEB/0] STDOUT at com.sap.cloud.mt.tools.impl.ServiceCallImpl.execute(ServiceCallImpl.java:187) ~[tools-2.7.0.jar:na]
2025-04-07T10:51:32.153+0000 [APP/PROC/WEB/0] STDOUT at com.sap.cloud.mt.tools.impl.ServiceCallImpl.lambda$execute$1(ServiceCallImpl.java:96) ~[tools-2.7.0.jar:na]
2025-04-07T10:51:32.153+0000 [APP/PROC/WEB/0] STDOUT at com.sap.cloud.mt.tools.impl.Retry.execute(Retry.java:37) ~[tools-2.7.0.jar:na]
2025-04-07T10:51:32.153+0000 [APP/PROC/WEB/0] STDOUT at com.sap.cloud.mt.tools.impl.ServiceCallImpl.execute(ServiceCallImpl.java:96) ~[tools-2.7.0.jar:na]
2025-04-07T10:51:32.153+0000 [APP/PROC/WEB/0] STDOUT at com.sap.cloud.mt.tools.impl.ServiceCallImpl.execute(ServiceCallImpl.java:74) ~[tools-2.7.0.jar:na]
2025-04-07T10:51:32.153+0000 [APP/PROC/WEB/0] STDOUT at com.sap.cloud.mt.subscription.SidecarAccess.callSidecarSubscribe(SidecarAccess.java:174) ~[multi-tenant-subscription-2.7.0.jar:na]
2025-04-07T10:51:32.153+0000 [APP/PROC/WEB/0] STDOUT at com.sap.cloud.mt.subscription.SubscriberSidecarImpl.lambda$subscribe$2(SubscriberSidecarImpl.java:80) ~[multi-tenant-subscription-2.7.0.jar:na]
2025-04-07T10:51:32.153+0000 [APP/PROC/WEB/0] STDOUT at com.sap.cloud.mt.subscription.MtxTools.subscribe(MtxTools.java:189) ~[multi-tenant-subscription-2.7.0.jar:na]
2025-04-07T10:51:32.153+0000 [APP/PROC/WEB/0] STDOUT at com.sap.cloud.mt.subscription.SubscriberSidecarImpl.subscribe(SubscriberSidecarImpl.java:79) ~[multi-tenant-subscription-2.7.0.jar:na]
2025-04-07T10:51:32.153+0000 [APP/PROC/WEB/0] STDOUT at com.sap.cds.services.mt.impl.MtSubscriptionServiceCompatibilityHandler.onSubscribe(MtSubscriptionServiceCompatibilityHandler.java:134) ~[cds-feature-mt-2.7.0.jar:na]
2025-04-07T10:51:32.153+0000 [APP/PROC/WEB/0] STDOUT ... 24 common frames omitted

the logs in plugin-demo-mtx:

2025-04-07T10:47:13.299+0000 [API/0] STDOUT Process became ready with guid 659357d5-a63d-4ccd-9c6a-d49f10e852e7 payload: {"instance"=>"5973745c-8c72-4141-46b6-f11b", "index"=>0, "cell_id"=>"29a3e68e-a187-42cf-814d-2c75ae4058a8", "ready"=>true, "version"=>"b8471e13-1bd1-4f75-83b5-783f67b5edee"}
2025-04-07T10:51:30.599+0000 [RTR/0] STDOUT sap-metering-metering-cap-test-org-plugin-demo-mtx.cfapps.sap.hana.ondemand.com - [2025-04-07T10:51:30.581819565Z] "POST /mtx/v1/internal/provisioning/subscribe HTTP/1.1" 404 1288 178 "-" "Apache-HttpClient/4.5.13 (Java/17.0.14)" "10.0.72.0:44068" "10.36.198.6:61325" x_forwarded_for:"52.28.114.230, 10.0.72.0" x_forwarded_proto:"https" vcap_request_id:"5154b910-8f98-49c3-5067-165ea5e8d1f3" response_time:0.017244 gorouter_time:0.000066 app_id:"659357d5-a63d-4ccd-9c6a-d49f10e852e7" app_index:"0" instance_id:"5973745c-8c72-4141-46b6-f11b" failed_attempts:0 failed_attempts_time:"-" dns_time:0.000000 dial_time:0.001850 tls_time:0.007496 backend_time:0.017178 x_cf_routererror:"-" x_correlationid:"1cb08e11-1f27-4b47-5e41-db1c0ac4a02e" tenantid:"-" sap_passport:"-" x_scp_request_id:"cf683639-cd81-4623-a270-da2177e692d1-67F3AE32-5D5DAC" x_cf_app_instance:"-" x_forwarded_host:"-" x_custom_host:"-" x_ssl_client:"-" x_ssl_client_session_id:"-" x_ssl_client_verify:"-" x_ssl_client_subject_dn:"-" x_ssl_client_subject_cn:"-" x_ssl_client_issuer_dn:"-" x_ssl_client_notbefore:"-" x_ssl_client_notafter:"-" x_cf_forwarded_url:"-" traceparent:"-" true_client_ip:"-" x_cf_true_client_ip:"52.28.114.230" x_request_id:"-" x_b3_traceid:"5154b9108f9849c35067165ea5e8d1f3" x_b3_spanid:"5067165ea5e8d1f3" x_b3_parentspanid:"-" b3:"5154b9108f9849c35067165ea5e8d1f3-5067165ea5e8d1f3"
2025-04-07T10:51:31.112+0000 [RTR/0] STDOUT sap-metering-metering-cap-test-org-plugin-demo-mtx.cfapps.sap.hana.ondemand.com - [2025-04-07T10:51:31.108964790Z] "POST /mtx/v1/internal/provisioning/subscribe HTTP/1.1" 404 1288 178 "-" "Apache-HttpClient/4.5.13 (Java/17.0.14)" "10.0.72.0:12630" "10.36.198.6:61325" x_forwarded_for:"52.28.114.230, 10.0.72.0" x_forwarded_proto:"https" vcap_request_id:"3ddf83b6-19d0-4d77-53e1-8319ff4835ce" response_time:0.003717 gorouter_time:0.000052 app_id:"659357d5-a63d-4ccd-9c6a-d49f10e852e7" app_index:"0" instance_id:"5973745c-8c72-4141-46b6-f11b" failed_attempts:0 failed_attempts_time:"-" dns_time:0.000000 dial_time:0.000000 tls_time:0.000000 backend_time:0.003664 x_cf_routererror:"-" x_correlationid:"1cb08e11-1f27-4b47-5e41-db1c0ac4a02e" tenantid:"-" sap_passport:"-" x_scp_request_id:"cf683639-cd81-4623-a270-da2177e692d1-67F3AE32-5D5E5F" x_cf_app_instance:"-" x_forwarded_host:"-" x_custom_host:"-" x_ssl_client:"-" x_ssl_client_session_id:"-" x_ssl_client_verify:"-" x_ssl_client_subject_dn:"-" x_ssl_client_subject_cn:"-" x_ssl_client_issuer_dn:"-" x_ssl_client_notbefore:"-" x_ssl_client_notafter:"-" x_cf_forwarded_url:"-" traceparent:"-" true_client_ip:"-" x_cf_true_client_ip:"52.28.114.230" x_request_id:"-" x_b3_traceid:"3ddf83b619d04d7753e18319ff4835ce" x_b3_spanid:"53e18319ff4835ce" x_b3_parentspanid:"-" b3:"3ddf83b619d04d7753e18319ff4835ce-53e18319ff4835ce"
2025-04-07T10:51:31.622+0000 [RTR/0] STDOUT sap-metering-metering-cap-test-org-plugin-demo-mtx.cfapps.sap.hana.ondemand.com - [2025-04-07T10:51:31.619192390Z] "POST /mtx/v1/internal/provisioning/subscribe HTTP/1.1" 404 1288 178 "-" "Apache-HttpClient/4.5.13 (Java/17.0.14)" "10.0.72.0:12696" "10.36.198.6:61325" x_forwarded_for:"52.28.114.230, 10.0.72.0" x_forwarded_proto:"https" vcap_request_id:"1e1c741d-5fa5-4c8f-4457-88548851fb2d" response_time:0.002815 gorouter_time:0.000086 app_id:"659357d5-a63d-4ccd-9c6a-d49f10e852e7" app_index:"0" instance_id:"5973745c-8c72-4141-46b6-f11b" failed_attempts:0 failed_attempts_time:"-" dns_time:0.000000 dial_time:0.000000 tls_time:0.000000 backend_time:0.002729 x_cf_routererror:"-" x_correlationid:"1cb08e11-1f27-4b47-5e41-db1c0ac4a02e" tenantid:"-" sap_passport:"-" x_scp_request_id:"cf683639-cd81-4623-a270-da2177e692d1-67F3AE33-5D5F26" x_cf_app_instance:"-" x_forwarded_host:"-" x_custom_host:"-" x_ssl_client:"-" x_ssl_client_session_id:"-" x_ssl_client_verify:"-" x_ssl_client_subject_dn:"-" x_ssl_client_subject_cn:"-" x_ssl_client_issuer_dn:"-" x_ssl_client_notbefore:"-" x_ssl_client_notafter:"-" x_cf_forwarded_url:"-" traceparent:"-" true_client_ip:"-" x_cf_true_client_ip:"52.28.114.230" x_request_id:"-" x_b3_traceid:"1e1c741d5fa54c8f445788548851fb2d" x_b3_spanid:"445788548851fb2d" x_b3_parentspanid:"-" b3:"1e1c741d5fa54c8f445788548851fb2d-445788548851fb2d"
2025-04-07T10:51:32.136+0000 [RTR/0] STDOUT sap-metering-metering-cap-test-org-plugin-demo-mtx.cfapps.sap.hana.ondemand.com - [2025-04-07T10:51:32.132977914Z] "POST /mtx/v1/internal/provisioning/subscribe HTTP/1.1" 404 1288 178 "-" "Apache-HttpClient/4.5.13 (Java/17.0.14)" "10.0.72.0:17566" "10.36.198.6:61325" x_forwarded_for:"52.28.114.230, 10.0.72.0" x_forwarded_proto:"https" vcap_request_id:"a5ed0237-d31e-4a43-509a-d56e043113b9" response_time:0.003021 gorouter_time:0.000062 app_id:"659357d5-a63d-4ccd-9c6a-d49f10e852e7" app_index:"0" instance_id:"5973745c-8c72-4141-46b6-f11b" failed_attempts:0 failed_attempts_time:"-" dns_time:0.000000 dial_time:0.000000 tls_time:0.000000 backend_time:0.002959 x_cf_routererror:"-" x_correlationid:"1cb08e11-1f27-4b47-5e41-db1c0ac4a02e" tenantid:"-" sap_passport:"-" x_scp_request_id:"cf683639-cd81-4623-a270-da2177e692d1-67F3AE33-5D6002" x_cf_app_instance:"-" x_forwarded_host:"-" x_custom_host:"-" x_ssl_client:"-" x_ssl_client_session_id:"-" x_ssl_client_verify:"-" x_ssl_client_subject_dn:"-" x_ssl_client_subject_cn:"-" x_ssl_client_issuer_dn:"-" x_ssl_client_notbefore:"-" x_ssl_client_notafter:"-" x_cf_forwarded_url:"-" traceparent:"-" true_client_ip:"-" x_cf_true_client_ip:"52.28.114.230" x_request_id:"-" x_b3_traceid:"a5ed0237d31e4a43509ad56e043113b9" x_b3_spanid:"509ad56e043113b9" x_b3_parentspanid:"-" b3:"a5ed0237d31e4a43509ad56e043113b9-509ad56e043113b9"

my .cdsrc.json file:

{
  "profiles": [
    "with-mtx-sidecar",
    "java"
  ],
  "requires": {
    "[local-multitenancy]": {
      "multitenancy": true
    },
    "[production]": {
      "db": "hana",
      "auth": "xsuaa",
      "multitenancy": true
    }
  }
}

my root package.json file:

{
  "name": "plugin-demo-cds",
  "version": "1.0.0",
  "description": "Generated by cds-services-archetype",
  "license": "ISC",
  "repository": "<Add your repository here>",
  "scripts": {
    "watch:mtx": "cds watch mtx/sidecar",
    "watch:java": "cd srv && mvn cds:watch",
    "build:cds": "cds build --for java",
    "subscribe": "cds subscribe t1 --to http://localhost:4005 -u alice: && cds subscribe t2 --to http://localhost:4005 -u bob:",
    "upgrade": "cds upgrade t1 --at http://localhost:4005 -u alice: && cds upgrade t2 --at http://localhost:4005 -u bob:"
  },
  "dependencies": {
    "@cap-js/sqlite": "^1.10.0",
    "@sap/cds": "^8.8.0",
    "@sap/cds-mtxs": "^2.7.0",
    "@sap/hdi-deploy": "^5.4.2",
    "@sap/xssec": "^4.5.0",
    "micromatch": "^4.0.8"
  },
  "resolutions": {
    "braces": "^3.0.3",
    "micromatch": "^4.0.8"
  },
  "devDependencies": {
    "@sap/cds-dk": "^8"
  }
}

my mta.yaml file:

_schema-version: 3.3.0
ID: plugin-demo
version: 1.0.0-SNAPSHOT
description: "A simple CAP project."
parameters:
  enable-parallel-deployments: true
modules:
  - name: plugin-demo-srv
    type: java
    path: srv
    parameters:
      instances: 1
      buildpack: sap_java_buildpack_jakarta
    properties:
      SPRING_PROFILES_ACTIVE: cloud,sandbox
      JBP_CONFIG_COMPONENTS: "jres: ['com.sap.xs.java.buildpack.jre.SAPMachineJRE']"
      JBP_CONFIG_SAP_MACHINE_JRE: '{ version: 17.+ }'
      JBP_CONFIG_JAVA_OPTS: "[from_environment: false, java_opts: '-javaagent:BOOT-INF/lib/opentelemetry-javaagent-2.2.0.jar -Dotel.metrics.exporter=logging
        -Dotel.javaagent.enabled=true -Dotel.java.global-autoconfigure.enabled=true -Dotel.traces.exporter=none -Dotel.logs.exporter=none
        -Dotel.instrumentation.common.default.enabled=false -Dotel.instrumentation.opentelemetry.api.enabled=true
        -Dotel.instrumentation.opentelemetry.instrumentation.annotations.enabled=true -Dotel.log.level=info']"
      CDS_MULTITENANCY_APPUI_TENANTSEPARATOR: "-"
    build-parameters:
      builder: custom
      commands:
        - mvn clean package -DskipTests=true --batch-mode
      build-result: target/*-exec.jar
    provides:
      - name: srv-api # required by consumers of CAP services (e.g. approuter)
        properties:
          srv-url: ${default-url}
    requires:
      - name: plugin-demo-auth
      - name: mtx-api
        properties:
          CDS_MULTITENANCY_SIDECAR_URL: ~{mtx-url}
      - name: plugin-demo-registry
      - name: app-api
        properties:
          CDS_MULTITENANCY_APPUI_URL: ~{url}
      - name: plugin-demo-db

  - name: plugin-demo
    type: approuter.nodejs
    path: app/router
    parameters:
      keep-existing-routes: true
      disk-quota: 256M
      memory: 256M
    requires:
      - name: srv-api
        group: destinations
        properties:
          name: srv-api # must be used in xs-app.json as well
          url: ~{srv-url}
          forwardAuthToken: true
      - name: plugin-demo-auth
      - name: mtx-api
        group: destinations
        properties:
          name: mtx-api # must be used in xs-app.json as well
          url: ~{mtx-url}
    provides:
      - name: app-api
        properties:
          app-protocol: ${protocol}
          app-uri: ${default-uri}
          url: ${default-url}
    properties:
      TENANT_HOST_PATTERN: "^(.*)-${default-uri}"

  - name: plugin-demo-mtx
    type: nodejs
    path: mtx/sidecar
    build-parameters:
      builder: custom
      build-result: gen
      commands:
        - npm run build
      requires:
        - name: plugin-demo-srv
    parameters:
      instances: 1
      memory: 256M
      disk-quota: 512M
    provides:
      - name: mtx-api
        properties:
          mtx-url: ${default-url}
    requires:
      - name: plugin-demo-db
      - name: plugin-demo-auth

resources:
  - name: plugin-demo-auth
    type: org.cloudfoundry.managed-service
    parameters:
      service: xsuaa
      service-plan: application
      path: ./xs-security.json
      config:
        xsappname: plugin-demo-${org}-${space}
        tenant-mode: shared
  - name: plugin-demo-registry
    type: org.cloudfoundry.managed-service
    requires:
      - name: srv-api
    parameters:
      service: saas-registry
      service-plan: application
      config:
        xsappname: plugin-demo-${org}-${space}
        appName: plugin-demo-${org}-${space}
        displayName: plugin-demo
        description: A simple CAP project.
        category: 'Category'
        appUrls:
          getDependencies: ~{srv-api/srv-url}/mt/v1.0/subscriptions/dependencies
          onSubscription: ~{srv-api/srv-url}/mt/v1.0/subscriptions/tenants/{tenantId}
          onSubscriptionAsync: true
          onUnSubscriptionAsync: true
          onUpdateDependenciesAsync: true
          callbackTimeoutMillis: 300000 # Increase if your deployments are taking longer than that
  - name: plugin-demo-db
    type: org.cloudfoundry.managed-service
    parameters:
      service: service-manager
      service-plan: container

entry.title: Issue with connection to postgresql from SAP CAP app in BAS
entry.content:
Hi Everyone,

when i run the command cds deploy, i get the following error:

$ cds deploy --tunnel-address localhost:5432
/> deployment to localhost:5432 failed

TimeoutError: ResourceRequest timed out

 

I do have the ssh tunnel running on port 5432, the PostgreSQL instance is working fine when i connect to it from my local machine via DBeaver but for some reason i can not deploy changes, when inspecting the env via "cds env" i do see the correct username, password and database name, so i really dont understand why i can not cds deploy my tables.

entry.title: SAP CAP how to skip persistence for all entities in a specific cds file?
entry.content:
Hi, i would like to skip persistence on all entities defined under my service.

As of right now i have to add @CDS.persistence.skip to each entity under service MyService { }, is there a better way to skip persistence for all entities under MyService?

Thanks

entry.title: CAP java sample app Manage Books Genre value help dialog does not show tree table data
entry.content:
Hi,

On running the sample app for cap java https://github.com/SAP-samples/cloud-cap-samples-java and navigating to Manage Books -> Create -> open value help on Genres field, we are not seeing the tree table data:

This is working with code from a couple of months back, but with the latest code we are seeing these errors. Can someone please help with this?

entry.title: DEPRECATED: req._queryOptions what to use to extract from query parameters?
entry.content:
Hello CAPlers,

I have productive use case to implement pagination with $skip, $top, $count from query parameter.

I must use functions which returns array of array of entity. Any pagination examples would be great to refer how the slicing logic is handled.
What should I use to make it work? 

When I use:

const { $top, $skip } = req._queryOptions || {};
I got 
------------------------------------------------------------------------------
DEPRECATED: req._queryOptions
Configuration req._queryOptions is deprecated and will be removed in upcoming releases!
------------------------------------------------------------------------------
Is there any documentation where it is mentioned? or maybe I missed
https://cap.cloud.sap/docs/node.js/events#cds-request

Please support. Thanks.

entry.title: Value Help for Input Parameter Field in Fiori Elements List Report + CAP
entry.content:
I'm trying to add value help for input parameters in a Fiori Elements List Report using OData V2 or V4 (I tried both, but without success). I need to use input parameters because my data comes from a parameterized view in the HANA database. For service/backend I'm using the CAP framework.

Getting both input parameters and properties to appear in the filter bar was already quite challenging. I only managed to get it working with OData V2.

Adding value help to the regular filter bar fields (based on entity properties) works fine, but I can't get it to work for input parameters.

Is there any known solution for this? Or maybe an example project on GitHub that shows how to implement value help for input parameters?

Thanks a lot!

entry.title: SAPUI5 : Extra PATCH call triggered on changing value of annotation based drop down.
entry.content:
We have observed a issue in Fiori Elements based List Report CAPM application for newer SAPUI5 versions > 1.120.0

We are currently on SAPUI5 version 1.134.0. We have defined annotation based drop down on object page as below and it renders fine with required values on UI.

Now every time we change value of this drop down, we observe an extra PATCH call triggered immediately on entity, trying to update the entity with this changed value of property referenced by dropdown, in this case firstName.

This causes wrong updating of entity value in database and if entity is defined @readonly, error is generated saying we are trying to update read-only entity.

Please guide on how to avoid this extra PATCH call or any workaround.

Please find below repo replicating the issue.

https://github.com/MudassirHasan1/ui5service

Regards.

entry.title: When to Update Node.js Based on the CAP Node.js Version?
entry.content:
We have a project in CAP that is currently on version 7.9.x (yes, we’re quite behind), and we plan to upgrade to the March 2025 release (8.9.x). From what I understand, the April release (9.x.x) will support Node.js versions 20 and 22. My question is, should we opt for Node.js 22, or stick with Node.js 20? Is the general recommendation always to use the latest Node.js version with the most recent CAP version?

Maybe this is a silly question, I read the documentation but I would like to know more about your opinion on whether the cap version works for two versions of Node.js. For example, version 20 and 22—my current version is 20, so when should I migrate to version 22 if version 20 still works?

entry.title: Accessing Product Description in Value Help Using sap/api_product/srvd_a2x/sap/product/0002 API
entry.content:
Hi SAP Community,

I'm working on an SAP CAP (Cloud Application Programming) project where I need to access product descriptions in a value help for a product using the OData API https://{host}:{port}/sap/opu/odata4/sap/api_product/srvd_a2x/sap/product/0002. However, I'm encountering an issue when implementing the search help for the product description, and I need some guidance.

To access the external API I've defined an index.cds file under /srv/external. In this file I have included the following entity projects:

namespace external;

@readonly
entity Product                               as
    projection on PRODUCT_0002.Product {
        key Product,
            BaseUnit,
            _ProductDescription  : redirected to ProductDescription,
    }

@readonly
entity ProductDescription                         as
    projection on PRODUCT_0002.ProductDescription {
        key Product,
        key Language,
            ProductDescription,
    }

In my service definition I've included the project following entities:

using {external as externalServices} from './external';

    entity Product                as projection on externalServices.Product;
    entity ProductDescription     as projection on externalServices.ProductDescription;

In the annotation for the fiori application I have included:

annotate service.x with {
    product @(
        Common.Label : '{i18n>product}',
        Common.ValueList : {
            $Type : 'Common.ValueListType',
            CollectionPath : 'ProductDescription',
            Parameters : [
                {
                    $Type : 'Common.ValueListParameterInOut',
                    LocalDataProperty : product_Product,
                    ValueListProperty : 'Product',
                },
            ],
        },
        Common.ValueListWithFixedValues : false,
    )
};

In the console I get the following error:

    message: 'Error during request to remote service: Unspecified provider error occurred. See Error Context and Call Stack.',
    code: 'ERR_BAD_REQUEST',
    config: {
      timeout: 0,
      xsrfCookieName: 'XSRF-TOKEN',
      xsrfHeaderName: 'X-XSRF-TOKEN',
      maxContentLength: -1,
      maxBodyLength: -1,
      method: 'get',
      baseURL: 'https://sandbox.api.sap.com/s4hanacloud/sap/opu/odata4/sap/api_product/srvd_a2x/sap/product/0002',
      proxy: false,
      url: "/Product(Product='undefined')?$expand=_ProductDescription($filter=Language%20eq%20'undefined')",
      data: undefined
    },

What am I missing here?

Thank you in advance for your time!

Nicolaas
